mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 151722 thousand in September 2019
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months with positive PAYEMS momentum over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE 3-month change exceeds 0.2pp over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and industrial production captures demand-supply coordination where consumer spending growth coincides with goods production expansion, amplifying earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 0 and 3-month change in INDPRO > 0
failure_condition: Positive joint signal instances produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a slowdown in nonfarm payrolls growth below its twelve-month trend signals exhaustion of labor market slack and impending demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth falls below 12-month trailing average growth
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 3.5% near half-century lows
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered months during late-cycle periods over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated short-term interest rates increase overnight funding costs and margin financing expenses, compressing speculative positioning and suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 2.04% in September 2019
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not negatively correlated with FEDFUNDS level (Rank IC > 0) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production momentum over multiple quarters predicts corporate earnings expansion and compression of equity risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated residential construction activity leads broader economic activity through multiplier effects on construction, materials, and consumer durables, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 1364k annual rate in August 2019
failure_condition: Positive HOUST changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation and rising unemployment erodes real corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4% and 12-month percent change in CPIAUCSL > 3%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered stagflationary periods over any 3-year rolling window.
